title: The Foundation: Hello World
intro: Welcome to Level 1. Every professional journey starts with the first line of code. We are setting up your workspace and understanding the basic building blocks of the web.

modules.title: What is JavaScript?
modules.summary: Slide 1: The Engine of the Web. JavaScript is a versatile programming language that brings life to websites. Why it matters? It is the only language that runs natively in every web browser. Slide 2: Definition. JavaScript is an interpreted, high-level language used for both client-side and server-side development. Slide 3: Example. You can change text, animate images, and process data without refreshing the page. Slide 4: Summary. JS is the most essential tool for any web developer today.

modules.title: Variables: const & let
modules.summary: Slide 1: Data Containers. A variable is a named storage for data. Slide 2: Why it matters? We need to keep track of user names, scores, and settings. Slide 3: Syntax. Always use 'const' for values that don't change and 'let' for values that will. Slide 4: Example. const pi = 3.14; let score = 0; Slide 5: Common Mistakes. Never use 'var' in modern JS because it can cause scope bugs. Slide 6: Summary. Use descriptive names and stick to ES6 variables.

title: Data Types & Precision
intro: Level 2 is about accuracy. In professional apps, data integrity is paramount. We'll master Types and basic math.

modules.title: Strings & Templating
modules.summary: Slide 1: Handling Text. Strings represent text in JS. Slide 2: Why it matters? Formatting user messages and building dynamic HTML sentences. Slide 3: Syntax. Use backticks for Template Literals to inject variables easily. Slide 4: Example. `Hello ${name}, welcome back!` Slide 5: Tip. Always escape quotes when not using template literals. Slide 6: Summary. Master strings to build interactive UIs.

modules.title: Numbers & Logic Flow
modules.summary: Slide 1: Math in Code. Numbers handle everything from counting to currency. Slide 2: Equality check. Professionals use triple equals for strict type checking. Slide 3: Syntax. Use comparison operators like greater than or less than. Slide 4: Common Pitfalls. Watch out for 'NaN' when doing math with undefined variables. Slide 5: Summary. Numbers are the backbone of application logic.

title: Decision Making: Logic
intro: Now we transition from simple storage to active logic. Level 3 focuses on making decisions with Conditionals.

modules.title: Conditionals (If/Else)
modules.summary: Slide 1: Branching Logic. Conditional statements act as the 'brain' of your code. Slide 2: Why it matters? Essential for showing 'Login' vs 'Profile' buttons. Slide 3: Syntax. The 'if' block checks a condition; 'else' handles the fallback. Slide 4: Practical Example. If age is over 18, allow access, otherwise show warning. Slide 5: Summary. Logic gates define the user experience.

modules.title: The Power of Loops
modules.summary: Slide 1: Automation. Loops repeat an action until a condition is met. Slide 2: Why it matters? Processing large lists of data without writing repetitive code. Slide 3: Syntax. The 'for' loop is the industry standard for controlled iteration. Slide 4: Common Trap. Avoid 'Infinite Loops' that crash your user's browser! Slide 5: Summary. Loops provide the scale for modern applications.

title: Data Collections: Arrays
intro: Level 4 is about managing groups of data. Arrays are the most common way to store lists of everything from products to users.

modules.title: Creating & Accessing Arrays
modules.summary: Slide 1: What is an Array? An array is a list-like object used to store multiple values in a single variable. Slide 2: Why it matters? Professionals use arrays to manage dynamic lists, like shopping carts or message feeds. Slide 3: Syntax. Arrays use square brackets and are zero-indexed. Slide 4: Example. const apps = ['Slack', 'Discord', 'VSCode']; Slide 5: Summary. Arrays are the foundation of data management in JS.

modules.title: Common Array Methods
modules.summary: Slide 1: Transforming Data. Methods like push, pop, and shift help you modify arrays. Slide 2: The .length property. Always know how many items are in your list. Slide 3: Practical Syntax. apps.push('Teams'); Slide 4: Pro Tip. Use .map and .filter for cleaner, modern code architectures. Slide 5: Summary. Mastering methods makes you a faster, more efficient developer.

title: Functional Excellence
intro: Level 5 is where you start writing reusable, professional code. We'll master Functions, Scope, and modern ES6 syntax.

modules.title: Declaring Functions
modules.summary: Slide 1: Reusable Logic. Functions are blocks of code designed to perform a specific task. Slide 2: Why it matters? DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) is the golden rule of professional engineering. Slide 3: Syntax. Define a function with parameters and return values. Slide 4: Example. function greet(name) { return `Hello ${name}`; } Slide 5: Summary. Functions are the building blocks of every application.

modules.title: Arrow Functions & ES6
modules.summary: Slide 1: Modern JS Syntax. Arrow functions provide a shorter syntax for writing function expressions. Slide 2: Why it matters? It is the industry standard in frameworks like React and Vue. Slide 3: Syntax. const add = (a, b) => a + b; Slide 4: Common Pitfalls. Be careful with 'this' keyword binding in arrow functions! Slide 5: Summary. Arrow functions make your code cleaner and more modern.

title: Bug Hunt: Debugging Expert
intro: Level 6 is a trial by fire. Professionals spend 70% of their time debugging. We'll start your first 'Bug Hunt' training here.

modules.title: The Infinite Loop Trap
modules.summary: There is a bug in the code below that crashes the browser. Find it and fix it.

modules.title: Chrome DevTools Pro Tips
modules.summary: Master breakpoints and the call stack. Real pros don't just use console.log.

title: Clean Code & Refactoring
intro: Level 8 is for architects. Writing code that works is easy; writing code that lasts is the real challenge.

modules.title: DRY & SOLID Principles
modules.summary: Maintainable code patterns used at Google and Meta.

modules.title: Refactoring Challenge
modules.summary: Refactor this messy nested logic into clean, readable functions.

title: The Professional: Mastery
intro: Final Level. You've reached the peak. Prove your mastery by building a production-ready application.

modules.title: System Design for Frontend
modules.summary: Scaling apps to millions of users.

modules.title: Final Capstone: AI Dashboard
modules.summary: Build a real-time AI status dashboard. This project will be the hero of your professional portfolio.
